channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Introducing Backlit: Lit SSR for Drupal, Hold the Node
item.description: Two years ago I wrote about server rendering Lit web components with Drupal. The approach worked, but it required a Node.js HTTP service running alongside Drupal in a container. That meant...

item.title: Most AI failures today are not model failures,they are control failures.
item.description: AI systems increasingly operate inside environments where decisions are not explicitly bounded. Without a defined Decision Boundary, systems continue execution beyond intended scope, often guided...

item.title: Building an Enterprise-Grade File Organizer with .NET and Avalonia UI
item.description: The Motivation We've all been there: the Downloads folder that looks like a digital jungle. I wanted to build something more than just a simple script; I wanted a robust, silent, and...

item.title: Avoiding malloc for Small Strings in C With Variable Length Arrays (VLAs)
item.description: Many C programs allocate small temporary buffers using malloc(), even when the buffer only lives for the duration of a function call. In many cases, stack allocation using VLAs can be...

item.title: How I Built Aegis — A Credential Isolation Proxy for AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are getting really good at calling APIs. But the way we usually give them access — pasting raw API keys into config files, env vars, or MCP settings — is broken from a security...

item.title: Building a Rails Engine #12 -- Beyond CSV: JSON and API Sources
item.description: Beyond CSV: JSON and API Sources CSV is the king of data import -- but in the real world, data also arrives as JSON from a file, or directly from a third-party API. Here is how DataPorter extends...

item.title: Web Pentesting Beginner Roadmap (2026): From Recon to Server-Side Attacks
item.description: The Web Pentesting Beginner’s Roadmap: From Recon to Server-Side A structured reference guide for anyone who just finished their first web security course. After completing the Hacksmarter Web...

item.title: Why You Should Use context.logger in Lambda Durable Functions
item.description: This article is a machine translation of the contents of the following URL, which I wrote in Japanese: Lambda Durable Functions のロギングには context.logger を使用しよう #Python - Qiita はじめに こんにちは、ほうき星...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Campaign Steals Crypto Seeds via Obfuscation, Chrome Exploit, and Social Engineering: Mitigation Strategies
item.description: Introduction & Threat Overview The GlassWorm malware campaign represents a critical evolution in cybercriminal tactics, specifically designed to exfiltrate cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases...

item.title: Why we chose Ceph as part of our storage-related solutions for production-scale AI
item.description: In the fast-paced world of DevOps and cloud infrastructure, there is a natural gravitation toward tools that offer instant gratification. We value the "Day 1" experience: the single binary...

item.title: Tubi and TikTok are partnering to produce long form series
item.description: Soon, some of TikTok's biggest personalities are going to be launching new streaming series on Tubi. Today, Tubi and TikTok announced that they are working together to launch a new Creatorverse...

item.title: Nothing Phone 4A Pro review: That flagship feeling
item.description: CEO Carl Pei says Nothing won't release a flagship phone this year. So instead we have the 4A Pro, a $499 phone that looks and feels higher-end than last year's flagship Phone 3, helped in large...

item.title: Adobe’s AI image generator can now be trained on your own art
item.description: Adobe is launching customizable AI image generators that can mimic specific artistic styles and character designs. The Firefly Custom Models are available in public beta starting today, allowing...

item.title: New Perseus Android Banking Malware Monitors Notes Apps to Extract Sensitive Data
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Android malware family called Perseus that's being actively distributed in the wild with an aim to conduct device takeover (DTO) and financial fraud....

item.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the UK
item.description: The company is currently letting users in the U.K. try out Alexa+ for free via an early access program.

item.title: Fitbit’s AI health coach will soon be able to read your medical records
item.description: Would you share your medical records with a personal trainer? How about a virtual one? Google, which this week announced it is giving Fitbit's AI health coach the ability to read your medical...

item.title: The Download: Quantum computing for health, and why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can...

item.title: Uber to invest $1.25 billion in Rivian as part of new robotaxi deal
item.description: Uber and Rivian are joining forces to deploy 50,000 fully autonomous robotaxis over the next several decades, the companies announced Thursday. As part of the deal, Uber will invest $1.25 billion...

item.title: How Ceros Gives Security Teams Visibility and Control in Claude Code
item.description: Security teams have spent years building identity and access controls for human users and service accounts. But a new category of actor has quietly entered most enterprise environments, and it...

item.title: A $5 million prize awaits proof that quantum computers can solve health care problems
item.description: I’m standing in front of a quantum computer built out of atoms and light at the UK’s National Quantum Computing Centre on the outskirts of Oxford. On a laboratory table, a complex matrix of...

item.title: Why the world doesn’t recycle more nuclear waste
item.description: The prospect of making trash useful is always fascinating to me. Whether it’s used batteries, solar panels, or spent nuclear fuel, getting use out of something destined for disposal sounds like a...

item.title: The Fight to Hold AI Companies Accountable for Children’s Deaths
item.description: After a series of suicides allegedly linked to AI chatbots, one lawyer is trying to hold companies like OpenAI accountable.

item.title: Hacking a Robot Vacuum
item.description: Someone tries to remote control his own DJI Romo vacuum, and ends up controlling 7,000 of them from all around the world. The IoT is horribly insecure, but we already knew that.

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Uses 6 Flaws, 3 Zero-Days for Full Device Takeover
item.description: A new exploit kit for Apple iOS devices designed to steal sensitive data from is being wielded by multiple threat actors since at least November 2025, according to reports from Google Threat...

item.title: Trump is threatening international students, and a new bill could help stop him
item.description: A bipartisan duo is pushing back on President Donald Trump's attempts to end a program that lets hundreds of thousands of foreign students work in the US for a year after graduation. Reps. Sam...

item.title: Multiverse Computing pushes its compressed AI models into the mainstream
item.description: After compressing models from major AI labs including OpenAI, Meta, DeepSeek and Mistral AI, Multiverse Computing has launched both an app that showcases the capabilities of its compressed models...

item.title: CISA Warns of Zimbra, SharePoint Flaw Exploits; Cisco Zero-Day Hit in Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has urged government agencies to apply patches for two security flaws impacting Synacor Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) and...

item.title: OpenAI to acquire Astral
item.description: Accelerates Codex growth to power the next generation of Python developer tools

item.title: Meta is having trouble with rogue AI agents
item.description: A rogue AI agent inadvertently exposed Meta company and user data to engineers who didn't have permission to see it.

item.title: Sam Altman’s thank-you to coders draws the memes
item.description: Altman expresses gratitude for people who knew how to write their code from scratch. The internet replies with salty jokes.
